mappings.rationaleAI: 20th-century architecture covers the impact of new materials like reinforced concrete and steel, and the rise of the skyscraper, directly relevant to construction materials, steel construction, and structural systems.
Vitruvius's De Architectura addresses building materials, constructional systems, and civil engineering including water supply, directly relevant to construction technology principles.
Brunel's Clifton Suspension Bridge, Thames Tunnel, and iron ships exemplify civil engineering works including bridges, underground construction, and steel/iron construction techniques.
Angkor Wat's engineering achievements including sophisticated water management systems relate to stone construction, building components, and hydraulic works.
Megalithic construction techniques using stone demonstrate early masonry construction methods for monumental building.
Robert Stephenson's Britannia Bridge and tubular bridge design represent major advances in bridge construction technology.
Glass science covers the history of glassmaking and modern applications, directly relevant to glass as a building material.
The Industrial Revolution covers iron and steel production, canal and railway construction including bridges and tunnels, relevant to construction materials and civil engineering works.
The Crystal Palace's revolutionary iron-and-glass architecture represents a landmark in steel and glass construction techniques.
